message:
refactor(ext4): split read_inode into raw + mode-checked variants for orphan/deletion walks

The orphan-list and deletion-walk loops in OpenFs were mis-using
`read_inode`, which now early-returns `FfsError::NotFound` when the
on-disk inode has `mode == 0`. That sentinel was added by the recent
ext4 inode-bitmap refactor (97ba1f4 / a360e5e), but it broke the
walks since their whole purpose is to traverse the chain of *deleted*
inodes (mode == 0 with `dtime` linking the next entry).

Fix by introducing a lower-level pair:

- `read_inode_raw_with_scope`: parses the on-disk inode without
  applying the mode==0 NotFound check.
- `read_inode_raw`: convenience wrapper that runs the raw read
  under the latest scope.

Then route the two orphan/deletion walks (around `next = inode.dtime`)
through `read_inode_raw` so they see deleted entries again, while
`read_inode` keeps its strict semantics for normal callers.

Also harden the compressed-cluster write paths
(`write_compressed_cluster` and `write_compressed_cluster_aligned`)
by re-acquiring `block_device_adapter()` after metadata-block
allocation. The previous code held a single immutable adapter for
the whole call; allocating new metadata blocks could invalidate
the cached block-device view and cause stale reads/writes during
the same call. The refresh sites are gated to the metadata-allocation
branches, so the fast path is unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
